title: zh-CN rewrite — 13 dogfood entries (M3 style)
description: Rewrite the 13 dogfood knowledge entries shipped in rc.2-prep from EN-only narrative to M3 style: EN section headings preserved, body paragraphs in zh-CN, EN technical terms (TypeScript, MCP, stable_id, layer-flip, etc.) preserved inline. Files: 8 KT-DEC decision entries (`.fabric/knowledge/decisions/KT-DEC-0001..0008.md`) + 5 init-scan baselines (tech-stack.md, module-structure.md, readme-first-paragraph.md, code-style.md, build-config.md). Frontmatter (id/type/maturity/layer) MUST remain unchanged — only body content is rewritten. doctor must still report clean after rewrite.

files.change: Rewrite body in zh-CN narrative, preserve EN section headings (## Decision, ## Alternatives considered, ## Rationale) and EN technical terms inline
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms
Rewrite body in zh-CN narrative; preserve EN section headings (## [MISSION_STATEMENT], ## [CONTEXT_INFO], etc) and EN technical terms (Node.js, TypeScript, pnpm, etc.)
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms (monorepo, packages/, etc.)
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings; quoted README content stays in original language
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms (ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript)
Rewrite body in zh-CN, preserve EN headings + tech terms (vite, tsconfig.json, pnpm-workspace.yaml)

implementation: Read all 13 files first to absorb current EN content — identify section headings (preserve), tech terms (preserve), narrative prose (rewrite)
For each KT-DEC-*.md: split file at frontmatter delimiter (`---`); preserve frontmatter byte-exact; rewrite body section-by-section

Per-section rewrite rule: keep EN heading verbatim (e.g. `## Decision`, `## Alternatives considered`, `## Rationale`); body becomes zh-CN narrative; preserve EN tech terms (TypeScript, MCP, stable_id, layer-flip, monotonic counter, async-review, etc.) inline; preserve code blocks unchanged
Style guidance: zh-CN body should be natural translation, not literal — match the conviction/density of EN original; keep paragraph boundaries; bullet points stay as bullets
After each file: verify frontmatter unchanged via `git diff` or `head -10` comparison
After all 13: run `node packages/cli/dist/index.js doctor` and confirm exit 0 (frontmatter still parses, schema still valid)
Run `pnpm test` — confirm any tests reading these files still pass (likely none directly, but watcher tests may touch)
Sanity check the `id`, `type`, `maturity`, `layer` frontmatter fields across all 13 files — must be unchanged from prior commit
Final convergence: each file's body contains zh-CN characters AND preserves at least 2 EN headings

reference.pattern: M3 language style — EN headings + zh-CN body + EN tech terms; per Q3 grill-locked decision
reference.examples: Section (g) of docs/data-schema.md (TASK-006) is the canonical reference for M3 style

criteria: All 13 files have zh-CN body — verify via `for f in $files; do file -b $f | grep -q UTF-8; done` AND grep for at least 5 zh-CN characters per file
EN section headings preserved — `for f in $files; do grep -E '^## [A-Z]' $f; done` returns ≥ 1 hit per file

Frontmatter unchanged — `git diff --stat $files | grep -E 'frontmatter|^---'` shows only body changes; or compare frontmatter blocks before/after via `awk '/^---$/{p=!p; if(!p)exit} p' file` round-trip
All 13 files still parse — `node packages/cli/dist/index.js doctor` exits 0
EN tech terms preserved — spot-check files contain at least 2 inline EN terms each (TypeScript, MCP, stable_id, etc.)
`pnpm test` exits 0 (no test regressions from file content change)
frontmatter id field across all 13 files matches stable_id pattern `K[PT]-(D|P|G|M|PR)-NNNN`

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: changing frontmatter fields (id/type/maturity/layer/created_at must stay byte-exact); creating new entries (TASK-009 only adds fabric-config); editing files outside the 13 listed; changing init.ts template (TASK-008); modifying docs (TASK-006). Strictly: body content rewrite of 13 specific files.

rationale.chosen_approach: M3 language style (EN headings + zh-CN body + EN tech terms) for monorepo dogfood entries to demonstrate the style locked in Q3 — fabric-monorepo eats its own zh-CN-default dogfood
alternatives_considered: Keep all EN (loses dogfood demonstration of language policy)
Full zh-CN including headings (rejected per Q3 — headings stay EN for searchability)
Per-file frontmatter override of language (rejected — config-level is the single source of truth)
decision_factors: Dogfood credibility — fabric-monorepo's own knowledge entries demonstrate the M3 style end-users will see
Q3 grill decision locked M3 style
Searchability preserved (EN headings + EN tech terms remain greppable)
rationale.tradeoffs: 13 files touched in one commit (~10-15 min per file translation) — accepted because all 13 share same style transition; alternative (per-file commits) would be ceremony-heavy

manual_checks: Eyeball KT-DEC-0001.md — confirm body reads naturally in zh-CN, EN headings intact, tech terms inline
Eyeball tech-stack.md — confirm same M3 pattern applied
Run `git diff --stat .fabric/knowledge/` — confirm 13 files changed, line counts roughly preserved (no major deletion)
Spot-check frontmatter on 3-4 files — confirm id/type/maturity/layer byte-exact unchanged
success_metrics: All 13 files have zh-CN body content
EN headings + EN tech terms preserved across all files
Reads naturally to a zh-CN reviewer
integration: frontmatter schema validation passes for all 13
